bullets: PP led the market sharply lower as NSRP cut its July offer deeply and high inventory amid slowing exports added pressure.
Cheap Korean cargo stays the cheapest in the market — including deeply-discounted off-grade material — keeping Chinese and domestic supply on the back foot.
Regional prices fell very sharply on the week amid persistent oversupply and wary, need-only buying.
PE kept sliding as Sabic settled its July offers well below early-week bids, confirming the downtrend.
Very cheap US and Korean cargo is resetting expectations lower, with some producers selling at a loss to clear stock.
Buyers stay cautious and need-driven, anticipating further cuts as feedstock pressure persists.
PVC held flat again, steadying after the week's sharp domestic cut, though sentiment remains fragile.
Regional benchmarks are unchanged on soft demand — a stability the market reads as weak, not firm.
Southern participants still expect erosion toward the 20,000 VND/kg mark next month if demand stays soft.

summary: Sabic settled LLDPE Film sharply lower at 1,030 CIF (-80 on week); injection 1,200, mLLDPE C6 1,250.
Korean Lotte LLDPE Film 1,000 CIF (960 for 800t+); cheap US Exxon LLDPE 930 CIF, close possible at 900.
LSP selling China-origin cargo at a loss to clear; out of own-plant PP/PE, LLDPE Film to 30,500 incl VAT.
paras: Today, June 26, SABIC's distributor announced its settlement selling prices for LLDPE cargo for July 2026 delivery to Vietnamese buyers, CIF from Singapore. Specifically, Sabic's LLDPE film settled at 1,030 USD/ton CIF this month, down sharply by 80 USD/ton versus the bid taken early this week; LLDPE injection settled at 1,200 USD/ton CIF, 50 USD/ton lower; and mLLDPE C6 settled at 1,250 USD/ton CIF, down 30 USD/ton versus the early-week bid. For goods purchased at bonded warehouses, the settled price is about 20 USD/ton higher than the quote.

Earlier, yesterday June 25, Sabic quoted a settlement price for LDPE Film to Vietnam at 1,300 USD/ton CIF, and settled LDPE coating at 1,400 USD/ton CIF — 60 USD/ton lower than the bid taken early this week. Sabic's HDPE settlement price has not yet been announced.

The distributor of Korea's Lotte this week quoted a settlement price for LLDPE Film to Vietnamese buyers at 1,000 USD/ton CIF; some buyers purchasing large volumes with orders over 800 tons were quoted a discount of up to 40 USD/ton, i.e. 960 USD/ton CIF. Lotte also took an HDPE yarn bid from Vietnamese buyers at 1,000 USD/ton CIF. Another Korean supplier, Hanwha, quoted an HDPE injection bid from Vietnamese buyers at 960-970 USD/ton CIF.

Some sources said a distributor settled HDPE yarn of PTT Thailand to Vietnamese buyers at 1,070 USD/ton CIF even though the quoted price held high at 1,200 USD/ton CIF. Another distributor offered HDPE yarn of Braskem Brazil to Vietnam at 1,020 USD/ton CIF.

In addition, the market noted some offers for US-origin goods at very competitive levels. For example, a distributor offered LLDPE Film 2018XBU/2018AY of Exxon Mobil US to Vietnam at 930 USD/ton CIF HCMC, with goods arriving in Vietnam around September-October 2026. A distributor said the supplier could settle this cargo at 900 USD/ton CIF. Some buyers revealed they were quoted US-origin HDPE Film at 940-950 USD/ton CIF, and US-origin HDPE blow offered at 960-980 USD/ton CIF... though the grade and delivery time remain unclear.

Domestically, the office of Long Son Petrochemical (LSP) this week settled HDPE Film FS7000 of STL China at 34,500 VND/kg incl. VAT, and settled LLDPE Film 7042 of Petrochina China at 31,200 VND/kg incl. VAT. According to the distributor, these are cargoes they accept selling at a loss because the import cost was considerably higher. Also per the LSP distributor, they have now run out of PP and PE inventory produced at their own plant on Long Son island; however, some southern market sources say LSP still has a quantity of LSP-origin LLDPE Film and is selling it to clear inventory at only 30,500 VND/kg incl. VAT.

On the commercial market, a large distributor lowered the offer for LLDPE Film 218WJ of Sabic to 33,000 VND/kg incl. VAT from mid-week, while some sellers lowered the offer for China-origin LLDPE Film to 32,000 VND/kg incl. VAT.

For HDPE, a distributor lowered the offer for HDPE Film F1 of Qamar to 36,000 VND/kg incl. VAT, up to 1,000-2,000 VND/kg lower than offers from some other traders, although HDPE F1 inventory is small and the import cost is high. US- and China-origin HDPE blow is offered at 34,000-35,000 VND/kg incl. VAT, though some buyers think the settled price could be only 33,000 VND/kg incl. VAT or below. PTT's HDPE yarn is noted with sellers offering at 38,500-39,000 VND/kg incl. VAT, but buyers are not willing to settle...

For LDPE goods, the offer for LDPE 260GG of Lotte Titan Malaysia has been lowered to 44,000 VND/kg incl. VAT, increasing downward price pressure on other LDPE grades. The LDPE price baseline across origins ranges from 41,000-44,000 VND/kg incl. VAT...

Across the wider region, per Platts pricing, in Northeast Asia the price baseline for HDPE film/yarn/blow/injection this week dropped sharply by 90 USD/ton versus the previous week; LLDPE fell 25 USD/ton; and LDPE fell 65 USD/ton. In Southeast Asia, the baseline for HDPE types also fell 50 USD/ton versus the previous week, ranging 1,180-1,190 USD/ton; LLDPE fell 20 USD/ton to 1,170 USD/ton CFR; LLDPE metallocene C6 fell 20 USD/ton to 1,320 USD/ton CFR; and LDPE fell 20 USD/ton to 1,320 USD/ton CFR.

The Asian PE market continued its downtrend this week under the impact of the falling crude oil and naphtha markets, combined with seasonally weak demand. In Southeast Asia, most market participants maintained a wait-and-see stance, buying mainly on actual demand, with the expectation that prices will continue to adjust downward when the Strait of Hormuz reopens.

summary: NSRP cut 114G July to 31,500 incl VAT (north quotes 31,000); BSR T3034 to 32,500, trades 32,000.
Korean Hanwha still cheapest: HJ500 30,000, off-grade HJ500Z just 28,000 incl VAT (300t sold north).
Platts SEA PP yarn/inj -95 to 1,095 CFR; regional oversupply with Korean cargo flooding in.
paras: A notable piece of news today is that a main distributor of Nghi Son Refinery (NSRP) lowered its offer for PP 114G for July delivery to 31,500 VND/kg incl. VAT, down 500 VND/kg versus the lowest settled price announced mid-week. Notably, in the north, some large distributors revealed they recorded a trading quote for the PP 114G July cargo at 31,000 VND/kg incl. VAT...

Sources say the NSRP plant is still running at maximum capacity, and PP inventory remains quite high as exports in the second half of June have slowed versus the first half of the month. NSRP's deep price cut for the July PP cargo has increased competitive pressure on the market.

BSR's PP T3034 today also recorded trading at 32,700-33,000 VND/kg incl. VAT in the HCMC market, although early this week most sellers were still offering at 33,800-34,000 VND/kg incl. VAT. A source says a large BSR distributor lowered its PP T3034 offer to 32,500 VND/kg incl. VAT for goods delivered at the plant warehouse in Quang Ngai, but customer interest remains low and the settled price could be only 32,000 VND/kg incl. VAT.

The Long Son Petrochemical (LSP) plant currently has no domestic PP inventory, only a small quantity of China-origin LLDPE and HDPE Film to offer. Vietnam's other remaining PP plant, Hyosung Vina, had not updated a new offer versus mid-week as of this morning, though some buyers say Hyosung's PP offers and trades are down a further 300-500 VND/kg versus early week (early this week, Hyosung Vina quoted PP injection J700N at 32,000 VND/kg excl. VAT, and PP block copo J640N at 35,000 VND/kg excl. VAT...).

The cheapest at the moment is still PP of Korea's Hanwha. A commercial seller offered PP HJ500 at only 30,000 VND/kg incl. VAT for a cargo arriving in early July, with room for further negotiation. In addition, some commercial sellers offered Hanwha PP HJ500Z (off-grade) at only about 28,000-28,500 VND/kg incl. VAT. A northern commercial seller shared that they sold nearly 300 tons of HJ500Z at 28,000 VND/kg incl. VAT, with goods delivered early July...
Hanwha PP copo BJ300, BJ350, and BJ550 are also noted offered at around 33,000-34,000 VND/kg incl. VAT, lower than the domestic PP copo offers of Hyosung or other origins...

Across the wider region, per Platts pricing, in Northeast Asia the baseline for PP yarn/injection this week dropped sharply by 90 USD/ton versus the previous week, IPP Film fell 125 USD/ton, and BOPP fell 130 USD/ton. In Southeast Asia, the baseline for PP yarn/injection this week also dropped sharply by 95 USD/ton to 1,095 USD/ton CFR, BOPP fell 95 USD/ton to 1,115 USD/ton CFR, and IPP Film and block copo fell 95 USD/ton to 1,145 USD/ton CFR.

According to pricing from another international organization, the PP baseline in Northeast Asia this week fell sharply by 90 USD/ton (with block copo down sharply to 1,110 USD/ton); the baseline for PP types in Southeast Asia fell uniformly by 50 USD/ton; and the baseline in India and other South Asian countries fell 70-80 USD/ton...

Thus, Asian PP prices dropped very sharply this week amid weak buying demand across the region. Market participants report an ongoing oversupply, while buyers are wary and limiting orders as prices keep falling day by day throughout the week. Producers are also proactively offering lower prices to lock in orders before the market falls further. In addition, the appearance of competitively-priced cargoes from Korea flooding into Southeast Asia is making Chinese suppliers hesitant to cut prices further to compete.

summary: Platts flat: China 750 / SEA 725 CFR — a 'weak stability' with soft downstream demand.
Domestic flat today after the deep AGC Vina PM66R cut to 20,900-21,000 incl VAT earlier in the week.
China operating rate ~70%; participants expect base prices toward 20,000 incl VAT next month.
paras: This week, the overall operating rate of PVC plants in China reached about 70%, edging up slightly versus the previous week. The increase came mainly from carbide-based PVC plants in the Northwest region as some previously idle units gradually restarted. Meanwhile, the operating rate of ethylene-based PVC remained low, around 50%, although some maintenance plants in the coastal area have begun to restart. This indicates that supply is generally recovering but has not yet created strong upward pressure.

In the Asian market, PVC prices last week generally held stable. Per Platts pricing, PVC CFR China held at 750 USD/ton, while PVC CFR Southeast Asia was flat at 725 USD/ton. However, this stability reflects a market lacking momentum more than a real balance, as downstream demand remains weak and buyers continue a cautious buying strategy based on actual demand while awaiting further price signals.

In the domestic market, after a sharp downward adjustment in the first session of the week when some southern traders deeply cut the offer for PVC PM66R of AGC Vina to 20,900-21,000 VND/kg (incl. VAT), the market today was temporarily flat. Even so, the price baseline remains under considerable pressure as the current offer is about 1,000 VND/kg lower than the mid-month factory selling price. This deep cut has negatively affected market sentiment, leading many southern participants to believe the downtrend is not over and that the price of oil-based PVC next month could fall back toward 20,000 VND/kg (incl. VAT) if demand remains sluggish.

On the import side, downward price pressure also continued. Early this week, a distributor offered Shintech's PVC SE950/SE1000 to the Vietnamese market at 670 USD/ton CIF, with the cargo expected to arrive at port in October 2026, down a further 20 USD/ton versus the mid-month settlement for the September-delivery cargo. This shows that foreign suppliers are still adjusting prices down to maintain competitiveness amid regional demand that has not yet clearly improved.
